old_value: {"id":"nayra-5hy","title":"BigInt TypeError in focus-manager.js crashes keyboard navigation","description":"handleEventChanges() at line 27 and findNearestEvent() at line 115 use `/ 2n` BigInt division, but point events have `end === undefined`. Subtracting BigInt from undefined throws 'Cannot mix BigInt and other types'. This fires on every store dispatch after focus is set, cascading to break: Tab navigation (focusNext/focusPrevious), search close on Escape (hide() dispatch crashes before display:none runs, search bar stuck open), zoom button callbacks (dispatch triggers same subscriber crash). Root cause: lines 27 and 115 assume all events have BigInt `end` values.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"charly.vibes@proton.me","created_at":"2026-03-30T13:35:29Z","created_by":"charly vibes","updated_at":"2026-03-30T13:35:29Z"}

new_value: Guarded BigInt midpoint calculation on lines 27 and 115 to fall back to event.start when end is undefined (point events)

old_value:
{"id":"nayra-g4t","title":"Dynamic lane height wastes canvas space and creates visual imbalance","description":"getDynamicLaneConfig in lane-positioning.js expands lane height up to MAX_LANE_HEIGHT=120px to fill available vertical space. With few lanes (1-2), this creates two problems:

**~90% empty canvas**: Events cluster in the bottom 10% near the axis. The upper canvas is unused dark space. Event Layout spec says 'layout SHALL use screen space efficiently'.

**Extreme point vs range disparity**: ISS Expedition 1 (range event) renders as ~25x120px cyan tower while adjacent point events are 4x4px dots in the same lane. The area ratio is ~500:1. Evidence in point-vs-range-contrast.png and iss-range-event.png.

The dynamic height is well-intentioned (filling space) but backfires with sparse data because:
- Point events don't scale with lane height (they stay 4px)
- The height expansion mostly adds whitespace inside the lane around the point event
- Range events dominate visually despite not being more important

Possible approaches: cap effective lane height lower for sparse datasets, scale point event size with lane height, or position lanes more centrally in the canvas rather than anchored to the bottom axis.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"charly.vibes@proton.me","created_at":"2026-03-30T13:49:01Z","created_by":"charly vibes","updated_at":"2026-03-30T13:49:01Z"}

new_value: Lowered MAX_LANE_HEIGHT from 120px to 40px so lanes don't balloon with sparse data, reducing the visual disparity between point and range events
